narrative.headline: 2020 Jeep Grand Cherokee: electrical issues lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 221 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 30%, followed by the engine (21%) and power train (17%). No deaths are reported. Air bags draw only 7% of complaints but account for the most crashes (11) and injuries (10) of any area, so check that system closely on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The biggest complaint area: 66 reports (30%), tied to 3 crashes. No fires or injuries reported here.

issues.sample: WITH MY FOOT COMPLETELY OFF THE ACCELERATOR, THE VEHICLE SPONTANEOUSLY ACCELERATES UNDER CERTAIN CONDITIONS. WHILE IN STANDARD OR SPORT MODE, IN SECOND GEAR GOING ABOUT 20MPH, THERE IS A REPRODUCIBLE RPM SURGE THAT CAUSES THE VEHICLE TO LUNGE FORWARD. THIS SOMEWHAT FEELS LIKE BEI
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 47 reports (21%), including 3 with fire. No crashes or injuries reported in this group.

issues.sample: WITH MY FOOT COMPLETELY OFF THE ACCELERATOR, THE VEHICLE SPONTANEOUSLY ACCELERATES UNDER CERTAIN CONDITIONS. WHILE IN STANDARD OR SPORT MODE, IN SECOND GEAR GOING ABOUT 20MPH, THERE IS A REPRODUCIBLE RPM SURGE THAT CAUSES THE VEHICLE TO LUNGE FORWARD. THIS SOMEWHAT FEELS LIKE BEI
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 38 reports (17%), linked to 2 fires, 2 crashes, and 1 injury. Worth checking how the vehicle behaves under acceleration.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 16 reports (7%), with 1 fire and 1 crash. A smaller but recurring complaint area.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 16 reports (7%) but the most serious by outcome: 11 crashes and 10 injuries. Check this system carefully.
